item.title: Canada defeat Fijiana to book semi final spot
item.description: The Fiji Airways Fijiana National 7s team suffered a quarter-final defeat to Canada 29-14 at the Bordeaux 7s today. Canada opened the scoring after controlling possession early and took a 7-0 lead.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Airways Fijiana National 7s team suffered a quarter-final defeat to Canada 29-14 at the Bordeaux 7s today.

Canada opened the scoring after controlling possession early and took a 7-0 lead.

Fiji responded through Mariana Talatoka, who produced a brilliant individual effort to score and level the match at 7-7.

However, Canada quickly regained momentum, dominating the rucks and breakdowns to win crucial possession.

Their pressure paid off with another try out wide, extending their advantage to 14-7.

Defensive lapses from Fiji allowed Canada to cross for another try before halftime, giving the North Americans a commanding 19-7 lead at the break.

Canada continued to control proceedings in the second half, adding 10 more points to their tally.

Fiji managed only one further try through Reapi Ulunisau, but it was not enough to challenge the Canadians.

Canada secured a convincing 29-14 victory and booked their place in the Cup semi-finals, while Fiji's Bordeaux campaign came to an end.

item.title: All New Zealand semi finals
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 07/06/2026 The Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific semi-finals have been confirmed, with the Hurricanes, the Crusaders, the Chiefs and the Blues securing their places following the qualifying finals.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 07/06/2026

The Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific semi-finals have been confirmed, with the Hurricanes, the Crusaders, the Chiefs and the Blues securing their places following the qualifying finals.

The Hurricanes thrashed the Brumbies 66-12, the Crusaders defeated the Blues 52-31, and the Chiefs beat the Reds 46-24.

Despite their loss to the Crusaders, the Blues claimed the final semi-final spot after recording the narrowest losing margin among the defeated qualifying finalists.

The Chiefs will host the Crusaders at 7.05pm Friday, while the Hurricanes will take on the Blues at 7.05pm Saturday.

item.title: Bula Boys suffer 2-1 defeat in friendly match
item.description: The Fiji Men’s National football team has suffered a 2-1 defeat to Vanuatu in their FIFA International Friendly despite a spirited

item.title: Yengi scores on debut as Socceroos hold Switzerland 1-1
item.description: Tete Yengi scored on his debut as Australia held Switzerland to a 1-all draw in their final warm-up match before the FIFA World Cup kicks off next Friday. Switzerland took the lead in the 14th minute through Dan Ndoye and dominated much of the first half.
item.content:encoded:
Tete Yengi scored on his debut as Australia held Switzerland to a 1-all draw in their final warm-up match before the FIFA World Cup kicks off next Friday.

Switzerland took the lead in the 14th minute through Dan Ndoye and dominated much of the first half.

However, the Socceroos responded after the break, with Yengi finding the equaliser in the 56th minute after a build-up involving Cameron Burgess and Connor Metcalfe.

Cristian Volpato also made his debut for Australia after switching allegiance from Italy, while Harry Souttar captained the Socceroos for the first time.

The result gives Australia a confidence boost ahead of the World Cup.

item.title: Chiefs through to semifinals after 46-24 win over the Reds
item.description: Navitalai Naivalurua · 07/06/2026 The Chiefs have booked their Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific semi-final spot after defeating the Reds 46-24 last night.
item.content:encoded:
Navitalai Naivalurua · 07/06/2026

The Chiefs have booked their Shop N Save Super Rugby Pacific semi-final spot after defeating the Reds 46-24 last night.

Damien McKenzie was influential for the Chiefs, scoring 24 points.

The Chiefs talisman crossed for two tries and added five conversions and two penalties.

Meanwhile, Kyren Taumoefolau scored a double, while Samisoni Taukei'aho and Isaac Hutchinson also crossed for tries.

For the Reds, flanker Fraser McReight, playing his 100th Super Rugby match, caused headaches for the home side at the breakdown, while scrum-half Tate McDermott impressed in just his third match of the season following a serious hamstring injury.

Hard-running centre Filipo Daugunu was the pick of the Reds' backs but did not receive as much ball as he deserved.

item.title: Iran accuses US of denying visas to football staff ahead of World Cup
item.description: Iran has accused the United States of denying visas to key members of its national football team's support staff ahead of the FIFA World Cup, despite players being granted entry. US officials confirms visas had been issued to Iranian players and necessary support staff but says the system would not be used to allow individuals into the country under false pretences.
item.content:encoded:
Iran has accused the United States of denying visas to key members of its national football team's support staff ahead of the FIFA World Cup, despite players being granted entry.

US officials confirms visas had been issued to Iranian players and necessary support staff but says the system would not be used to allow individuals into the country under false pretences.

Iran's embassy in Turkey claims several managerial staff, technical advisers and football officials were denied entry and called the move politically motivated interference in sport.

The embassy has also urges FIFA to intervene, while Iranian media reports say the head of the football federation and his deputy were among those refused visas.

The issue comes just days before Iran's opening World Cup match in Los Angeles on June 15th.

The 2026 FIFA World Cup, co-hosted by the United States, Canada and Mexico, which begins next Friday.

item.title: Fijiana survive Brazil scare to seal thrilling 28-24 win
item.description: The Fiji Airways Fijiana side produced a thrilling performance against Brazil, holding firm under pressure to claim a 28-24 win in their final pool match. Fiji struck first when Atelaite Ralivanawa pounced on a loose ball from the Brazilians to open the scoring. However, Brazil hit back quickly to level the contest just minutes later.
item.content:encoded:
The Fiji Airways Fijiana side produced a thrilling performance against Brazil, holding firm under pressure to claim a 28-24 win in their final pool match.

Fiji struck first when Atelaite Ralivanawa pounced on a loose ball from the Brazilians to open the scoring. However, Brazil hit back quickly to level the contest just minutes later.

Reapi Ulunisau then crossed over to restore Fiji’s lead at 12-7, but Brazil responded with sustained pressure and multiple phases to draw level again, sending both teams into halftime tied at 14-14.

The Brazilians came out strong in the second half, scoring two quick tries to surge ahead 25-14. Ulunisau once again stepped up for Fiji, scoring to bring the deficit back to 24-21.

With momentum shifting, Fiji dug deep and found another crucial try to complete the comeback and survive a fierce Brazilian challenge, sealing a hard-fought 28-24 victory.

Th e Women's Cup quarter-finals are set to commence at 1:30am today.

In other results Spain defeated Great Britan 29-10, USA beat Canada 33-22 and Australia defeated Japan 26-7.

Fijiana will face Canada at the quarter final.

item.title: Crusaders run riot in 52-31 win over Blues
item.description: Sevu Reece scored a try to help his Crusaders team to a book their place in the Super Rugby Pacific semi-finals with a commanding 52-31 victory over the Blues at One New Zealand Stadium. After trailing 7-0 early, the Crusaders responded through Reece before taking control following a red card to Blues forward Malachi Wrampling midway through the first half.
item.content:encoded:
Sevu Reece scored a try to help his Crusaders team to a book their place in the Super Rugby Pacific semi-finals with a commanding 52-31 victory over the Blues at One New Zealand Stadium.

After trailing 7-0 early, the Crusaders responded through Reece before taking control following a red card to Blues forward Malachi Wrampling midway through the first half.

Tries from Johnny McNicholl, David Havili, Chay Fihaki, and Taha Kemara helped the hosts build a dominant 33-14 halftime lead.

The Crusaders maintained their momentum after the break, with McNicholl completing a hat-trick and Manumaua Letiu adding another try as they ran in eight tries overall.

Despite late scores from Xavi Taele, Payton Spencer and Caleb Clarke, the Blues were unable to close the gap as the Crusaders sealed an emphatic 52-31 win.

item.description: Anything but another home Crusaders playoff win would be a surprise.Indeed, the defending champions’ remarkable run in home playof
